message:
feat(wizard): add centralized step validation before navigation (bd-2gys)

Add validation framework for wizard steps:
- ValidationResult type and optional validate() on WizardStep interface
- Step 1 (OS selection) validates with auto-detection fallback
- Step 5 (VPS creation) validates IP address entry
- useStepValidation hook manages error state, scroll-to-focus, auto-dismiss
- Error banner in layout.tsx with role="alert" for accessibility
- Forward navigation validates; backward always allowed (don't block exploration)

Replaces hardcoded validation in layout.tsx (ensureOSSelected, IP check)
with extensible per-step validation system.

Closes bd-2gys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2fov","title":"JFP: Add TLDR entry + stats alignment","description":"Add JeffreysPrompts (jfp) to the TLDR quick reference data.\n\nScope:\n- Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts: add a JFP tool entry (prefer core category) with description, synergies (ms/apr), and commands.\n- Update tldrPageData hero description/stats/supportingDescription to match actual tool counts after adding JFP.\n\nValidation:\n- bun run build (apps/web) if convenient; otherwise note not run.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:45:27.345267808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:50:08.093983957Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:50:08.093915138Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2gog","title":"Add doctor.sh health checks for all utility tools","description":"## Task

Add basic health checks to scripts/lib/doctor.sh for all 9 utility tools.

## Checks to Add

Each utility should have at minimum a binary existence check:

| Tool | Check Command |
|------|---------------|
| tru | command -v tru |
| rust_proxy | command -v rust_proxy |
| rano | command -v rano |
| xf | command -v xf |
| mdwb | command -v mdwb |
| pt | command -v pt |
| aadc | command -v aadc |
| s2p | command -v s2p |
| caut | command -v caut |

## Implementation Pattern

Use the existing check() function pattern:
```bash
check "util.tru" "tru (token notation)" "$(command -v tru &>/dev/null && echo pass || echo warn)" "optional utility"
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 9 utilities have health checks
2. Checks are non-fatal (warn, not fail) since utilities are optional
3. Doctor runs without errors
4. Proper categorization in doctor output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:33.424346589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:10:12.798643984Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","health-check","utilities"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gog","depends_on_id":"bd-1ega","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:10:12.798545559Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2gog","depends_on_id":"bd-1ega.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:47.793624868Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2gys","title":"Step validation in web wizard before navigation","description":"## Overview
Add pre-navigation validation in the web wizard to ensure required fields are filled before moving to the next step.

## Current Problem
- Users can click "Next" without completing required actions
- Leads to confusion in later steps that depend on earlier choices
- No feedback about what's missing

## Proposed Behavior
1. Each step defines validation rules
2. "Next" button disabled until validation passes
3. Clear visual indication of incomplete fields
4. Helpful error messages explaining what's needed

## Validation Rules by Step
- OS Selection: Must select Ubuntu or Debian
- VPS Provider: Must select provider (optional: show comparison)
- SSH Setup: Must have valid SSH key path or generate new
- Install Options: At least one module selected
- etc.

## Implementation Details
1. Add `validate()` function to each wizard step config
2. Create `useStepValidation` hook
3. Validation runs on field change and before navigation
4. Store validation state in wizard context

## Component Changes
```tsx
// wizardSteps.ts
export interface WizardStep {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  slug: string;
  validate?: (state: WizardState) => ValidationResult;
}

interface ValidationResult {
  valid: boolean;
  errors: string[];
}
```

## UX Considerations
- Don't block exploration (allow viewing future steps)
- Clear distinction between "incomplete" and "invalid"
- Inline validation messages, not just on submit
- Persist partial progress to localStorage

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test each step's validation rules
- [ ] Test "Next" disabled when invalid
- [ ] Test error messages display correctly
- [ ] Test validation on field blur
- [ ] Test localStorage persistence of partial state

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/lib/wizardSteps.ts (add validate functions)
- apps/web/components/wizard/WizardNavigation.tsx
- apps/web/hooks/useStepValidation.ts (new)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:36.402265120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:36:02.074761758Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gys","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:43.392191184Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2h1y","title":"Deep exploration: XF (X Archive Search)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of XF (X/Twitter Archive Search) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify XF installation
[[ -d /dp/xf ]] && echo "PASS: xf repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: xf repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v xf &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: xf command available" || { echo "FAIL: xf not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check for archive data
[[ -d ~/.local/share/xf/archive ]] && echo "INFO: XF archive exists" || echo "INFO: No archive yet"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/xf-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/xf/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for archive format docs, search syntax

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Archive import process (from X/Twitter export)
  - Index building (SQLite FTS5?)
  - Search query syntax
  - Content types supported (tweets, DMs, likes, Grok)
  - Output formats (human, robot)

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
xf --help 2>&1 | head -20
xf search --help 2>&1 | head -10
xf import --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
xf search "test" --limit 3 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `cass search 'xf twitter archive' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions
- Check for XF usage patterns in existing sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/xf/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/xf && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Archive import mechanism
- [ ] Index structure (FTS5 schema)
- [ ] Search query syntax (operators, filters)
- [ ] Content types (tweets, DMs, likes, bookmarks, Grok)
- [ ] Robot output format

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] cass - similar search patterns
- [ ] brenner - research integration
- [ ] cm - memory of twitter insights

### 2.3 Archive Verification
```bash
# Check archive contents
ls -la ~/.local/share/xf/ 2>/dev/null | head -10

# Count indexed items
xf stats 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No stats command"

# Check supported content types
xf types 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No types command"
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update xf entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: X/Twitter archive search
- `description`: Full-text search capabilities
- `deepDescription`: How archive indexing works
- `features`: Verified content types and search
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test xf entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const xf = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'xf');
console.log('Testing xf entry...');
console.assert(xf, 'xf entry exists');
console.assert(xf.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(xf.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Archive Search
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/xf-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== XF E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Basic search
echo "Test 1: Basic search..." | tee -a $LOG
xf search "test" --limit 3 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Robot mode
echo "Test 2: Robot mode..." | tee -a $LOG
xf search "code" --robot --limit 3 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 3: Content type filter (if available)
echo "Test 3: Filter test..." | tee -a $LOG
xf search "agent" --type tweets --limit 3 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No type filter"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-2h1y --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Content types documented correctly
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:02:52.507929572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T21:36:49.700800407Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2p56","title":"acfs changelog: Show recent changes command","description":"## Overview
Add `acfs changelog` command that shows recent changes, new tools, and breaking changes since last update.

## Current Problem
- Users don't know what changed after `acfs update`
- No visibility into new features
- Breaking changes can surprise users
- Manual changelog reading is friction

## Proposed Output
```bash
$ acfs changelog
ACFS Changelog (since your last update: 2026-01-20)

## 2026-01-25
- ✨ NEW: Added `caam` (Coding Agent Account Manager)
- 🔧 FIX: Checksum verification for atuin installer
- 📝 DOC: Improved SSH setup guide

## 2026-01-22  
- ⚠️ BREAKING: Renamed `acfs doctor --verbose` to `--json`
- ✨ NEW: Added dark mode to web wizard
```

## Implementation Details
1. Maintain CHANGELOG.md in repo root
2. Parse changelog to extract entries
3. Filter by date (since last update)
4. Format for terminal output
5. Support `--json` for machine parsing

## Changelog Format (Keep-a-Changelog)
```markdown
## [2026-01-25]
### Added
- `caam` tool for account management

### Fixed
- Checksum verification for atuin

### Changed
- Doctor output format
```

## Commands
```bash
acfs changelog              # Since last update
acfs changelog --all        # Full history
acfs changelog --since 7d   # Last 7 days
acfs changelog --json       # Machine-readable
```

## State Tracking
- Store last_update_version in state.json
- Compare against current version
- Show "You're up to date!" if no new changes

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test changelog parsing
- [ ] Test date filtering
- [ ] Test --json output format
- [ ] Test "up to date" message
- [ ] Test with malformed changelog

## Files to Create
- scripts/commands/changelog.sh
- CHANGELOG.md (if not exists)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:27.680404290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:09:57.324341410Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2p56","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:35.745684631Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qgg","title":"Deep exploration: UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/ubs-preflight.log
echo "=== UBS Pre-flight Check: $(date) ===" | tee $LOG

[[ -d /dp/ultimate_bug_scanner ]] && echo "PASS: Directory exists" || { echo "FAIL: Directory not found"; exit 1; }
[[ -f /dp/ultimate_bug_scanner/README.md ]] && echo "PASS: README exists" || echo "WARN: No README"

# Check if ubs is installed
if command -v ubs &>/dev/null; then
  echo "PASS: ubs command available: $(which ubs)" | tee -a $LOG
  ubs --version 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG
else
  echo "WARN: ubs command not in PATH" | tee -a $LOG
fi
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/ubs-exploration-snapshots
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
```

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
```bash
cat /dp/ultimate_bug_scanner/README.md
ls -la /dp/ultimate_bug_scanner/patterns/ 2>/dev/null  # Check pattern files
ls -la /dp/ultimate_bug_scanner/rules/ 2>/dev/null
```

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Architecture: ast-grep patterns, how they're loaded
- Language handlers: count actual supported languages
- Detection categories: COUNT actual categories (verify 18 claim)
- Output formats: JSON, JSONL, SARIF - verify each works
- Pre-commit hook: how it integrates
- Performance: measure actual scan time

### 1.3 Verify Claims
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== UBS Claim Verification ===" | tee /tmp/ubs-claims.log

# Count detection categories
CATEGORY_COUNT=$(ls /dp/ultimate_bug_scanner/patterns/*.yaml 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
echo "Detection categories found: $CATEGORY_COUNT (claimed: 18)" | tee -a /tmp/ubs-claims.log

# Count supported languages
LANG_COUNT=$(grep -r "language:" /dp/ultimate_bug_scanner/patterns/ 2>/dev/null | sort -u | wc -l)
echo "Languages found: $LANG_COUNT (claimed: 7+)" | tee -a /tmp/ubs-claims.log

# Test performance claim (sub-5-second)
START=$(date +%s.%N)
ubs scan /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib --json >/dev/null 2>&1
END=$(date +%s.%N)
DURATION=$(echo "$END - $START" | bc)
echo "Scan time: ${DURATION}s (claimed: <5s)" | tee -a /tmp/ubs-claims.log
```

### 1.4 External Context
```bash
/xf search 'ubs OR "ultimate bug scanner" OR ast-grep' 2>&1 | head -50
cass search 'ubs bug scanner' --robot --limit 10 2>&1
```

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE)

Document with VERIFICATION:
- [ ] Detection categories: ACTUAL count = ___ (vs 18 claimed)
- [ ] Languages: ACTUAL list = ___ (vs 7+ claimed)
- [ ] Performance: ACTUAL time = ___ (vs <5s claimed)
- [ ] Output formats: JSON ✓/✗, JSONL ✓/✗, SARIF ✓/✗
- [ ] Synergies VERIFIED:
  - [ ] bv: [actual integration?]
  - [ ] slb: [actual integration?]
  - [ ] dcg: [actual integration?]
- [ ] Tech stack: ACTUAL language = ___

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE)

### 3.1 Update with VERIFIED claims only
- If 18 categories cannot be verified, use actual count
- If 7 languages cannot be verified, list actual languages
- Only list synergies that are VERIFIED

### 3.2 Cross-reference Validation
```bash
for tool in bv slb dcg; do
  grep -A10 "id: '$tool'" /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts | grep -q "ubs" && echo "PASS: $tool lists ubs" || echo "WARN: $tool missing ubs"
done
```

## Phase 4: Testing

### 4.1 Static Analysis
```bash
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web
bun run type-check 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ubs-typecheck.log
bun run lint 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ubs-lint.log
```

### 4.2 Build
```bash
bun run build 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ubs-build.log
[[ $? -ne 0 ]] && cp /tmp/ubs-exploration-snapshots/*.before apps/web/lib/ && exit 1
```

### 4.3 E2E Visual
```bash
lsof -t -i :3000 | xargs kill 2>/dev/null; sleep 2
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web && bun run dev &
sleep 10
curl -sL http://localhost:3000/flywheel | grep -qi 'ubs' && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"
curl -sL http://localhost:3000/tldr | grep -qi 'ubs' && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"
kill %1
```

### 4.4 Unit Tests
```bash
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web
grep -q "id: 'ubs'" lib/flywheel.ts && echo "PASS: ubs exists" || echo "FAIL"
diff -q lib/flywheel.ts /tmp/ubs-exploration-snapshots/flywheel.ts.before && echo "WARN: unchanged" || echo "PASS: changed"
```

### 4.5 CLI Tests
```bash
ubs --help 2>&1 | head -20 || echo "FAIL: ubs --help failed"
ubs scan --help 2>&1 | head -20 || echo "FAIL: ubs scan --help failed"
```

### 4.6 Results Log
```bash
RESULTS=/tmp/ubs-exploration-test-results.log
echo "=== UBS RESULTS ===" > $RESULTS
cat /tmp/ubs-claims.log >> $RESULTS
grep -E "PASS|FAIL|WARN" /tmp/ubs-*.log >> $RESULTS
FAIL_COUNT=$(grep -c "FAIL" $RESULTS); echo "FAILURES: $FAIL_COUNT" >> $RESULTS
cat $RESULTS
```

## Phase 5: Commit

```bash
[[ $(grep -c "FAIL" /tmp/ubs-exploration-test-results.log) -gt 0 ]] && exit 1

cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
git add apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
git commit -m "docs(flywheel): update UBS descriptions with VERIFIED claims

- Verified detection category count: [actual]
- Verified language support: [actual list]
- Verified performance: [actual timing]
- Synergies verified against implementations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

br update bd-2qgg --status closed
br sync --flush-only && git add .beads/ && git push
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pre-flight passed
- [ ] All claims VERIFIED (not assumed)
- [ ] Detection categories: verified count
- [ ] Languages: verified list
- [ ] Performance: verified timing
- [ ] All tests PASS
- [ ] Content de-slopified
- [ ] Pushed

@@ -889,8 +889,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-31ps","title":"EPIC: ACFS no-brainer improvements (manifest sync + support + UX)","description":"## Goal
Deliver the 10 most obviously accretive improvements identified via idea-wizard: manifest-driven web content, support bundle, structured install logging, wizard command builder, manifest-driven doctor checks + fix hints, doctor --fix, preflight network health, pinned-ref installer UX, module failure resume hints, and a web tool-status page.

## Why
These are low-risk, high-impact improvements that reduce drift, improve debuggability, and make the beginner UX more deterministic. They align with ACFS’s single-source-of-truth philosophy and reduce support burden.

## Scope
All changes remain within existing architecture: manifest + generator, install.sh orchestration, scripts/lib, and apps/web. No new backends.

## Non-Goals
- No re-architecture of installer phases
- No new external services
- No breaking changes without flags

## High-Level Deliverables
1. Manifest to web generated data (tools/tldr/commands/lessons index).
2. acfs support-bundle command (logs + state + doctor JSON + versions).
3. Structured install logs + JSON summary artifacts.
4. Wizard command builder with shareable URL state.
5. Doctor uses generated checks + per-module fix suggestions.
6. acfs doctor --fix for safe deterministic fixes.
7. Preflight network + repo health checks.
8. Pinned ref UX (--pin-ref + wizard toggle).
9. Auto-resume hint on module failure.
10. Web tool status page generated from manifest.

## Success Criteria
- Docs and generator stay in sync.
- Support/debugging becomes one-command reproducible.
- Wizard reduces copy/paste errors.
- acfs doctor provides actionable fixes and optional auto-fix.
- All changes have unit tests and e2e scripts with detailed logging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:03.423708833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:03.423708833Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.1","title":"Manifest-driven web content generation (tools/tldr/commands/lessons)","description":"## Background
Website tool lists (flywheel.ts, tldr-content.ts, commands.ts, lessons index) are hand-maintained and drift from acfs.manifest.yaml. ACFS’s single-source-of-truth goal implies the manifest should drive web content to eliminate duplication.

## Goal
Generate web-facing tool/lesson data from the manifest so the website always reflects the real install set and metadata.

## Scope
- Extend manifest schema/types with web metadata fields (display_name, short_desc, category, docs_url, lesson_slug, tldr_snippet, command_examples, etc.).
- Update generator to emit apps/web/lib/generated/*.ts.
- Refactor web app to consume generated data.

## Considerations
- Must keep backward compatibility with existing content; provide migration adapters.
- Generated files live under apps/web/lib/generated and are NEVER edited manually.
- Keep web bundle size stable (tree-shake generated modules).

## Testing
- Generator unit tests for deterministic output.
- Web type-check + build.
- Web E2E smoke for pages that use generated data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No manual edits needed in flywheel.ts/tldr-content.ts/commands.ts/lessons.ts for tool lists.
- A single manifest change updates web content after `bun run generate`.
- CI passes (lint/type-check/build).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:32.712577003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:32.712577003Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:32.712577003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.1.1","title":"Manifest web metadata: schema + types + validation","description":"## Goal
Extend manifest schema + types to include web-facing metadata needed for generated website content.

## Tasks
- Define `web` metadata fields in Module (e.g., display_name, short_desc, docs_url override, category_label, tldr_snippet, command_example, lesson_slug, visibility flags).
- Update packages/manifest/src/types.ts and schema.ts with validation rules (string lengths, optional fields).
- Update parser/validator to surface warnings for missing recommended web fields on first-class tools.
- Document defaults and migration strategy (if web fields omitted, fall back to existing content).

## Considerations
- Keep fields optional to avoid breaking existing manifest entries.
- Avoid overfitting: start with minimal set needed for current pages.
- Ensure schema constraints prevent unsafe content (no HTML injection).

## Tests
- Unit tests for schema validation (valid/invalid).
- Snapshot tests for parse + validate warnings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema accepts existing manifest with no changes.
- New web metadata fields validate correctly.
- Warnings emitted for missing recommended fields on critical modules.","notes":"Implemented ModuleWebMetadata: types.ts interface (20 optional fields), schema.ts Zod validation (hex color, kebab-case icon, href path regex, bounded lengths), parser.ts warnings for web-visible modules missing recommended fields, full exports in index.ts. 170 tests pass. Backwards compatible.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:52.551265569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:29:56.695662534Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:29:56.695596660Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:52.551265569Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-31ps.1.2","title":"Generator: emit web data modules","description":"## Goal
Generate web-facing data modules from acfs.manifest.yaml.

## Tasks
- Extend packages/manifest/src/generate.ts to emit apps/web/lib/generated/*.ts (tools, tldr, commands, lessons index).
- Define stable output contracts: types + naming + export shape.
- Ensure generated files include a header warning and are deterministic (sorted output).
- Update generator tests to assert output stability and to cover new data files.

## Considerations
- Keep generated output small and tree-shakable.
- Prefer pure data (no React imports).
- Keep cross-repo path resolution robust in generator (uses repo root).

## Tests
- generate.test.ts: new fixtures for web outputs.
- generate:diff shows expected changes only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `bun run generate` creates/updates web generated files.
- Output is deterministic across runs.
- No manual edits needed in generated files.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:20:18.485787766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:31:10.552564560Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:20:18.485787766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:39:34.487137375Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-31ps.1.3","title":"Web app: consume generated tool/lesson data","description":"## Goal
Refactor apps/web to consume generated data modules instead of hand-maintained lists.

## Tasks
- Replace manual arrays in flywheel.ts, tldr-content.ts, commands.ts, lessons.ts with generated imports and small adapters if needed.
- Ensure categories, labels, and ordering match current UI expectations.
- Provide fallback for any missing web metadata (e.g., use module description).

## Considerations
- Avoid breaking existing routes and tests.
- Keep component props unchanged where possible.
- Ensure tree-shaking works and no circular imports.

## Tests
- Web type-check and lint.
- Playwright smoke on /tldr, /learn, /flywheel pages.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No direct edits to generated files.
- UI renders same or better content with generated data.
- Build succeeds with no regressions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:20:40.917316830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:39:56.235231830Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:20:40.917316830Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:39:56.233300772Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.1.4","title":"Tests: manifest → web generated content","description":"## Goal
Add explicit automated coverage for manifest-driven web generation.

## Tasks
- Generator tests: snapshot the generated web data outputs for a known manifest fixture.
- Web tests: Playwright smoke tests for pages that depend on generated data (flywheel/tools/tldr/learn).
- CI: ensure `bun run generate` runs before web build in test scripts.

## Logging
- For web E2E: reuse tests/web/run_e2e.sh structured logging and save artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail if generated web output drifts.
- Web E2E runs validate critical pages render without runtime errors.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:21:16.575137558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:41:10.567428544Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:21:16.575137558Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:40:36.615197145Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:41:10.565648781Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.2","title":"Add acfs support-bundle command (diagnostics pack)","description":"## Background
Support/debugging requires scattered manual steps (logs, state.json, doctor output, versions). A single support bundle simplifies triage and reduces back-and-forth.

## Goal
Add an `acfs support-bundle` command that collects diagnostics into a timestamped archive with redaction.

## Scope
- New script in scripts/lib/support.sh.
- New CLI entrypoint via acfs command dispatcher.
- Bundle includes: state.json, install logs, doctor --json, versions, OS info, selected config files, recent errors.

## Considerations
- Redact tokens/secrets (API keys, auth tokens).
- Size limits and opt-in for large logs.
- Output should be safe to share.

## Testing
- Unit tests for redaction + file collection.
- Docker E2E: create bundle and verify contents.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One command produces a shareable bundle with manifest + logs + doctor JSON.
- Redaction verified and documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-31ps.2.1","title":"Support-bundle implementation + CLI wiring","description":"## Goal
Implement the support-bundle collection logic and CLI entrypoint.

## Tasks
- Create scripts/lib/support.sh with functions: collect_files, capture_doctor_json, capture_versions, capture_env_summary.
- Add `acfs support-bundle` command routing (acfs wrapper / doctor.sh dispatch table).
- Output bundle directory: ~/.acfs/support/<timestamp>/ plus .tar.gz archive.
- Include a manifest JSON describing included files and versions.

## Considerations
- Ensure stdout stays clean for piping; write progress to stderr via logging.sh.
- Avoid requiring root; use sudo only when needed for logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `acfs support-bundle` produces a tarball + manifest JSON.
- Bundle includes doctor JSON and state.json when present.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:37.265721256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:22.566950225Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:37.265721256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:41:52.853108258Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:22.564866490Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.2","title":"Support-bundle redaction rules + tests","description":"## Goal
Define and implement redaction rules for the support bundle.

## Tasks
- Identify sensitive patterns: API keys, auth tokens, bearer headers, .env secrets, OAuth refresh tokens.
- Implement redaction in support.sh (regex-based, with allowlist for safe values).
- Provide a summary report of redactions performed.
- Add a `--no-redact` flag for advanced users (with warning).

## Tests
- Unit tests: known secrets replaced with <REDACTED>.
- Ensure false positives are minimal (e.g., Git SHAs not redacted).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle safe to share by default.
- Redaction summary included in manifest JSON.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:55.389196201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:55.389196201Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:55.389196201Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.3","title":"Tests: acfs support-bundle","description":"## Goal
Add automated coverage for support-bundle generation.

## Tasks
- Unit tests for collection functions (mock files + redaction).
- Docker E2E: run `acfs support-bundle`, verify tarball contains expected files + manifest JSON.
- Log output to /tmp/acfs_support_bundle_test_TIMESTAMP.log with pass/fail summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail if required bundle components are missing.
- E2E run produces detailed logs + summary.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:05.075169407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:51.658165458Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:05.075169407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:39.178930848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:51.657404163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.4","title":"Docs: support-bundle usage + privacy","description":"## Goal
Document support-bundle usage and privacy expectations.

## Tasks
- Add README section describing `acfs support-bundle` and where output is stored.
- Include redaction notes + how to disable with --no-redact.
- Add troubleshooting guidance on what to attach when filing issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can find and run the command easily.
- Clear warning about sensitive data and redaction behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:22.919954847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:22.919954847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:22.919954847Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
